section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: October 23rd, 2025

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: Ekhoscènes is the leading French employers' union at national level, for the private live show industry (music, variety shows, theater, cabarets). Since 1984, Ekhoscènes' mission has been to represent, promote, and defend the interests of its members. Ekhoscènes brings together live show organizers covering the entire chain of creation and distribution of a live show in France.

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: Our contribution will focus on consumer protection in the online tickets distribution. The relationship between performing arts professionals and consumers begins with the purchase of a ticket, frequently online, which is the right allowed by the show organizer to the spectator to access the venue and attend the live show. The price of this access right is determined by the live show organizer with the artist.

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: Ekhoscènes wishes to share its observations on two important topics for live shows organizers:

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: Scams involving the online distribution of access rights to live shows

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: Dynamic pricing

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: Scams involving the online distribution of the access rights to live shows

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: 1.1 The access rights to live shows are not goods

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: An access right to a live show (commonly referred to as a "ticket") is the proof (instrumentum) of the contract established between the live show organizer and the consumer who will attend said live show.

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: This legal qualification was reaffirmed by the European Court of Justice (ECJ) in a ruling dated March 31, 2022. The ECJ confirmed that the relationship between a live show organizer and the spectator constitutes a service contract.

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: The object of this contract is "the transfer of the right of access to the leisure activity shown on the tickets" (§ 33). The ECJ further clarified that "such a contractual relationship [...] essentially concerns the transfer of a right and not of goods" (§ 34) and that the ticket is the "document" that "sets down this right" (§ 35).

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: 23, rue du Renard – 75004 Paris

section_path: Feedback from Ekhoscènes regarding spectators protection in the on-line distribution of live shows access rights
paragraphs.text: La voix des entrepreneurs du spectacle vivant privé

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: Access to live shows is a "service", and tickets are obviously not "goods". A ticket has no value in itself. What has value is the show that the ticket grants access to. In this contribution, we thus designate the tickets by the correct legal term "access rights".

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.2 The live show organizer must control the distribution of access rights to their live shows
paragraphs.text: Unlike the distribution of goods – which is a series of sales contracts – the distribution of the right to obtain a service (here, attending a live show) requires an uninterrupted contractual link between the service provider (the live show organizer) and the final customer (the spectator).

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.2 The live show organizer must control the distribution of access rights to their live shows
paragraphs.text: Today, the access right to a live show acquired by a spectator is an electronic data shared by all parties involved in the distribution of access rights. For the spectator, this data is materialized by a barcode or QR code (on an electronic or printed medium, if the spectator chooses to print it), which only has authenticity if the information it bears is recognized during the access control. Same applies today for booking reservations in a hotel, train or plane.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.2 The live show organizer must control the distribution of access rights to their live shows
paragraphs.text: In order to know how many access rights are to be marketed, how many have been marketed at a given time, and how many remain to be marketed, and to ensure the reliability of this information up to the access control in the venue on the day of the live show, it is essential that the data flows in an organized manner from the live show organizer to the venue, passing through various distributors and sub-distributors.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.2 The live show organizer must control the distribution of access rights to their live shows
paragraphs.text: Thus, just like the distribution of train or plane tickets, museum accesses, or hotel bookings, the distribution of access rights for live shows, online or offline, must be controlled end-to-end by the service provider (carrier, entertainment venue, hotel-keeper, live show organizer). For example, when a consumer books a hotel room through a travel agency (the agency being either physical or online), the consumer is certain that the hotel and the room booked do exist and are available for reservation because there is, between the consumer and the hotel-keeper, an uninterrupted chain of contractual relationships, organized and controlled by the service provider, i.e., the hotel-keeper.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.2 The live show organizer must control the distribution of access rights to their live shows
paragraphs.text: Therefore, the marketing of access rights must necessarily follow an organized distribution chain from the live show organizer to the spectator, passing through each distributor and sub-distributor.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.3 The live show industry is an economy of scarcity
paragraphs.text: Live shows, like sports events, are organized with an irreducible constraint: the limited capacity of the venue where they take place.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.3 The live show industry is an economy of scarcity
paragraphs.text: It will never be possible to sell more access rights to the live show than the number of seats available in the venue or stadium, nor to accommodate more spectators than this physical and insurmountable limit.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.3 The live show industry is an economy of scarcity
paragraphs.text: Just as sports competitions cannot be replayed (a game happens only once), live shows cannot be performed indefinitely (the artists cannot perform on stage until they have satisfied the entire audience). These are

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.3 The live show industry is an economy of scarcity
paragraphs.text: La voix des entrepreneurs du spectacle vivant privé

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: limits inherent to human beings, including celebrities. In movie theaters, a film can be shown as long as there is an audience. This is not possible for a live show.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: By nature, the ticketing market is a market of scarcity for highly popular live shows.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: By nature, the ticketing market deals with non-substitutable services: the spectator wants to attend the live show of a specific artist, on a specific day, in a specific city, and not the live-show of another artist.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: By nature, there will always be disappointed spectators who cannot acquire access rights to a live show, regardless of the marketing techniques that the live show organizer and their distributors may implement.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: For the past fifteen years, spectators have been victims of operators who act in violation of the legal framework of access rights distribution. These players take advantage of the inevitable scarcity of access rights to the most in-demand events to pretend they have tickets and scam consumers.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: When a spectator purchases its access right from an operator not authorized by the live show organizer, for instance through websites pretending to be authorized distributors (while they are not) or pretending to be "platforms" or "marketplaces" claiming to connect individuals, the purchaser has no guarantee of being able to attend the live show for which they believed they had paid for:

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: the access right is often a fake that has not been issued by the relevant live show organizer, preventing entry to the venue;

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: the access right may also be a duplicate of another access right, and a first has already accessed the venue;

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: the live show does not exist;

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: the spectator does not get the information should the live show be postponed or cancelled;

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: etc.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: These practices violate existing European consumer law and the national law of each country (contract law, property law, criminal law, etc.).

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: Some member states have also introduced specific national legislations penalising such behaviour within the European Union.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: Courts or administrative sanctions are often taken in France, Italy, Germany, Spain and other members states, against operators involved in the illegal marketing of live shows access rights, to the detriment of live shows organizers, artists and, of course, consumers.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: 23, rue du Renard – 75004 Paris

paragraphs.text: Ekhoscènes

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: La voix des entrepreneurs du spectacle vivant privé

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: However, as highlighted in the call for evidence, there is a lack of enforcement of this existing legislation. This is the key issue. These violations of the law go unpunished due to a lack of action by the public authorities and a lack of resources for the justice system in each member state and at European Union level.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: Ekhoscènes and its members are particularly committed to the fight against operators who illegally market access rights to the live shows they produce, and this has been the case for many years.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: This fight should receive much more support from public authorities both at the national and EU levels. It is the resources of public authorities that need to be strengthened in a coordinated manner at European level.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: Effective and rapid systems for taking websites offline or social networks pages, accounts or posts where consumer law violations are committed should be duly applied.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: Another challenge faced by our members is the enforcement of the court rulings they obtain when the convicted companies are not established in France. Even within the European union, in criminal proceedings for breaches of consumer law, cooperation mechanisms do not always work between member states, consumer protection is not being taken as a priority compared to other criminal offences.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.4 The fight against illicit access rights resellers
paragraphs.text: Enforcement is even harder where companies are located outside the European Union. Every company offering services to access live shows taking place in Europe should have registered offices within the European Union.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: When access rights for live shows by renowned artists go on sale, announcements are usually published in the media a few weeks or days before the official opening date of the sale. This communication aims to inform fans and consumers, but it also attracts the attention of offenders who take advantage of high demand to engage in illicit practices.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Among the main methods used is the creation of websites marketing fakes access rights to the shows. The illicit operators design websites that imitate the appearance of the live show organizer brads. Their aim is to scam consumers by pretending to sell access rights to these shows whereas they have no distribution agreements with the live show organizer.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Today, given the low-cost, easy-to-use e-commerce website creation tools available today online, such as, for example, Shopify.com, it is extremely easy for scammers to set up websites, collect online payments for sometimes considerable amounts, and thus engage in completely illicit activity with no risk.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: This is made even easier by the fact that these online e-commerce website creation services offer integrated payment solutions, allowing scammers to easily obtain payments from their victims.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: The first element of fairness in online commerce is that websites accepting payments are operated by operators acting lawfully on the market.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: 23, rue du Renard – 75004 Paris

section_path: Ekhoscènes

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Ekhoscènes

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: La voix des entrepreneurs du spectacle vivant privé

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Thus, it is necessary to regulate providers who enable the creation of these illicit websites by requiring them to verify the legality of the activities offered on the e-commerce websites they allow to create. A KYC process should be imposed to such service providers who are the "entry door" for offenders and frauds to consumer rights.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Then, when such an illicit website is identified, it should be possible to have it removed or blocked immediately and effectively, without going through lengthy and expensive legal proceedings.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Dynamic pricing is just another method of determining the price of access rights to a live show

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: The call for evidence mentioned "dynamic pricing" as a practice that may require targeted prohibition.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Dynamic pricing can be used to determine the selling price of tickets to a show.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Dynamic pricing is already regulated by European consumer law. European consumer law requires professionals to provide consumers with clear and comprehensible information on the total price of the service inclusive of taxes or, where the price cannot reasonably be calculated in advance, the manner in which the price is to be calculated (Art. 6 1. e) of the Consumer Rights Directive).

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: For this practice to comply with consumer law, it is necessary:

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: To inform consumers about the process that will take place before their purchase;

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: That the consumer remains free not to purchase once the final price is known and indicated.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: Under these conditions, the practice of dynamic pricing cannot be considered unfair practices. No additional regulation is required. It is only necessary to strengthen the enforcement of existing legislation and effectively penalise non-compliance by illegal operators.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: The European Commission, in its response on September 27, 2024, to the question raised by two Members of Parliament specifically regarding the price of access rights to live shows, reiterated: "Dynamic pricing is not prohibited by EU consumer protection law. Companies can freely determine the prices they charge as long as they adequately inform consumers about the total price."

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers
paragraphs.text: The live show organizers, like any supplier of goods or services in a free-market economy, are free to determine the price of their services and the method by which this price is set, in accordance with existing consumer protection laws."

section_path: Ekhoscènes
1.5 The use of digital technology by illicit operators to scam consumers

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: La voix des entrepreneurs du spectacle vivant privé

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: Only access right distribution controlled by the live show organizer and set up with trusted commercial partners will enable every spectator to access a show, within a fair distribution circuit and with secure physical access to the venue.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: Operators interfering in the marketing of tickets for live shows without the authorization of the organizers of these live shows commit unfair and misleading commercial practices, violate the contract and property laws of member states, and commit criminal offences under the national law of member states.

section_path: Ekhoscènes
paragraphs.text: These practices are detrimental to live shows organizers, artists and consumers alike. They also generate major safety risks for spectators and venue staff, when thousands of abused people turn up with invalid tickets.
